title: elaeagnus conferta subsp. javanica
intro: Elaeagnus conferta subsp. javanica, also known as the Javan Olive, is a species of flowering plant in the family Elaeagnaceae. This plant has been used in traditional medicine to treat a variety of ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The health benefits of Elaeagnus conferta subsp. javanica include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and exhibit antioxidant activity, making it a potential natural remedy for various health issues. Herbal preparations of this plant can be found in the form of infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat various conditions, although it is essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using any herbal remedy.
